div.head: The Chargé in France (Armour) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Sir: With reference to this Embassy’s despatch No. 10007 of November 18, 1929,Not printed. enclosing a copy and translation of a note from the Foreign Office in which it was stated that the Société Industrie et Commerce du Fruit Conservé par le Sucre had been advised semiofficially that it would be to its interest to give up trade-mark No. 148177, bearing the American flag, I have the honor to report that I am in receipt of a further note from the Foreign Office, dated November 21, 1929, in regard to this matter. In this note it is stated that the company has replied that it was its intention to register before the Tribunal de Commerce de la Seine a renunciation of the trade-mark in question and as soon as possible to dispose of all containers and labels on which it is reproduced.
